rules.rationale: L-MIND excluded primary-refractory disease, so there is no efficacy evidence for tafasitamab-lenalidomide in this setting; the exclusion is by disease biology, not age or transplant-ineligibility.
Autologous transplant benefits only chemotherapy-sensitive relapse (PARMA); chemorefractory disease removes ASCT from the differential.
For fit patients with primary-refractory or early-relapse (<=12 mo) LBCL, 2L CD19 CAR-T is superior to salvage chemo plus ASCT (ZUMA-7, TRANSFORM).
Liso-cel is a 2L option for transplant-ineligible patients regardless of relapse timing (PILOT), with lower CRS/ICANS than axi-cel.
Tisagenlecleucel failed its second-line trial (BELINDA, no EFS benefit); it is not a second-line option, unlike axi-cel and liso-cel.

Only epcoritamab monotherapy is approved (3L+); the GemOx combination and 2L use are off-label but prospectively supported (EPCORE NHL-2). Name the boundary, cite the basis and its limits, and let the physician proceed.

Glofitamab+GemOx received a US Complete Response Letter (STARGLO) and is not FDA-approved; glofitamab is US-approved only as monotherapy at 3L+. Flag as off-label/unavailable, do not present as approved standard.
Bendamustine before apheresis impairs T-cell fitness and cellular-therapy outcomes; do not give a bendamustine-containing regimen when CAR-T or a T-cell engager is still intended.

rules.therapy: CD19-directed therapy (tafasitamab, loncastuximab) before CD19 CAR-T
rules.rationale: Sequencing a CD19-directed agent before a planned CD19 CAR-T raises antigen-loss/escape concern; flag the sequencing and confirm target-antigen strategy.
Polatuzumab's MMAE payload compounds pre-existing (vincristine-related) peripheral neuropathy; flag when grade >=2 neuropathy is reported.

There is no numeric age cutoff for CAR-T; eligibility is a composite of physiologic fitness, organ function, comorbidity, caregiver reliability, and logistics. Represent 'too old for CAR-T' only as a failing composite, never as an age rule.
For patients aged >=70, ECOG alone under-detects vulnerability; require geriatric-assessment domains (ADL/IADL, falls, cognition, polypharmacy, comorbidity index) before calling a patient fit.

Before labeling a case double-hit, require the MYC FISH method: true double-hit needs a MYC translocation (rearrangement), not amplification/copy-number gain. Block auto-upgrade of 'double-hit-like' to true double-hit.

rules.therapy: CNS-penetrant systemic backbone (HD-MTX / high-dose cytarabine / thiotepa)
rules.rationale: Parenchymal secondary CNS disease requires CNS-penetrant systemic therapy; intrathecal-only or R-ICE-type regimens are insufficient because they do not reach parenchymal disease. R-DHAP (cytarabine) is more CNS-active than R-ICE.
When CAR-T is chosen with baseline CNS disease, prefer lower-ICANS products (liso-cel/tisa-cel); the basis is TRANSCEND and registry data, not a routine on-label CNS indication.
Copanlisib was withdrawn from the US market (2023) and has weak/uncertain CNS penetration; it is not a valid current bridging option.

High-dose methotrexate requires adequate renal function, absence of third-spacing (ascites/effusions), reconciliation of clearance-delaying drugs (NSAIDs, PPIs, penicillins), and a leucovorin-rescue/hydration/alkalinization plan.

Bispecific CRS/ICANS risk is front-loaded to cycle-1 step-up; require ramp-up at the certified center for distant patients and hand-back to the local oncologist only after the ramp-up window. Flag any plan that assigns the highest-risk doses to the community.
CAR-T and bispecific ramp-up require a reliable caregiver who can recognize and report CRS/ICANS and return promptly; flag when caregiver support is absent or unreliable.

Do not down-rank an active regimen solely because the disease is GCB or not double-hit when it is primary-refractory; primary-refractoriness is the dominant adverse driver, and under-treating a fit patient risks a foreseeable poor outcome.

Preserve real-world diagnostic uncertainty: when serial imaging and clinical picture are convincing, an equivocal or necrotic re-biopsy does not veto treatment; do not require histologic confirmation to proceed. (Clinical-practice standard.)

R-miniCHOP is a validated age-attenuated first-line regimen for patients >=80; early progression after it reflects refractory biology, not inadequate dosing, and should not down-triage subsequent intent.
